supply_chain.dependencies: Nearly all footwear and apparel products are manufactured outside the United States by independent contract manufacturers. Concentration risk with top footwear contract manufacturers accounting for approximately 59% of production. Reliance on Tier 2 material suppliers for principal materials.
supply_chain.risks: Concentration of manufacturing in Vietnam (51% footwear), Indonesia (28% footwear), and China (17% footwear). Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical conflicts, tariffs, trade policies, pandemics. Difficulty in sourcing raw materials during periods of high demand or shortages. Dependence on third-party logistics and transportation. Factory input costs exposed to foreign currency fluctuations.

regulatory_risks: Import duties, quotas, sanctions, political and economic instability, conflicts and terrorism
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and other anti-bribery laws compliance
Changes in U.S. trade policies including tariffs and trade agreement enforcement
New tariffs imposed in 2025 expected to materially increase Cost of sales
Trade restrictions and restrictions on transfer of funds
Product safety regulations around the world
Corporate responsibility and sustainability regulations (greenhouse gas emissions disclosure, diversity program limitations)
Data privacy laws (GDPR in EU/UK, state laws in US, Data Security Law and Personal Information Protection Law in China)
Income tax law changes (OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax, US tax reforms)
Employment regulations (wage and hour laws, minimum wage, overtime, citizenship requirements)
European Commission State Aid investigation in Netherlands regarding tax rulings
Belgian Customs claims for alleged underpaid duties

sanction_risks: Sanctions and countersanctions affecting international trade
Economic sanctions on individuals, corporations or countries
Restrictions on imports/exports due to geopolitical conflicts
Political tensions, instability, conflicts, nationalism and terrorism affecting operations
US-China trade tensions and retaliatory measures
Geopolitical conflicts disrupting supply chain and manufacturing
